title: Electron Backscatter Diffraction (EBSD) — crystallographic orientation mapping
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (genesis fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical electron-microscopy literature for this principle.

E.description: Electron Backscatter Diffraction (EBSD) — crystallographic orientation mapping: kikuchi diffraction pattern produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.excitation.electron_beam -> L.diffraction_kikuchi -> L.pattern_match -> int.angular, with multi-angle tomographic integration and photon-shot-noise-limited (Poisson counting). Recovery is posed as a non-convex inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side euler angle field. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~25; calibration-level mismatch (pattern_center_drift, band_contrast_degradation, grain_boundary_ambiguity) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: I_kikuchi(theta, phi; orientation) = pattern_library(orientation); match per pixel to assign Euler angles
E.world_state_x: Scene parameter (density / orientation / spectrum / complex field depending on principle)
E.observation_y: Electron detector output at declared geometry + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered euler angle field is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness is local rather than global (non-convex landscape); convergence depends on initialisation and priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 25); pattern_center_drift dominates the stability cliff; band_contrast_degradation and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Photon-shot-noise-limited (poisson counting) sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while TV / wavelet-sparsity / deep priors stabilise recovery at the ill-conditioned end of Omega.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [Hough-EBSD, Dictionary-Match] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [EBSD-Net]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sqrt(sigma_signal^2) / sqrt(photon_count). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |pattern_center_drift| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
